verses.sanskrit: तम् दृष्ट्वा परम प्रीतो विश्वामित्रो महाबलः ।प्रणतो विनयात् वीरो वसिष्ठम् जपताम् वरम् ॥१-५२-१॥
verses.text:
On seeing at that best sage among best meditators Sage Vashishta, the great-mighty and valorous Vishvamitra 
is highly rejoiced and obediently made an obeisance to the sage. [1-52-1]

verses.sanskrit: स्वागतम् तव च इति उक्तो वसिष्ठेन महात्मना ।आसनम् च अस्य भगवान् वसिष्ठो व्यादिदेश ह ॥१-५२-२॥
verses.text:
The great-souled Vashishta spoke, "you are welcome," and that godly sage has indeed shown a high seat 
to Vishvamitra. [1-52-2]

verses.sanskrit: उपविष्टाय च तदा विश्वामित्राय धीमते ।यथा न्यायम् मुनि वरः फल मूलम् उपाहरत् ॥१-५२-३॥
verses.text:
The eminent saint Vashishta then customarily offered fruits and tuber to the courageous Vishvamitra 
who by now has assumed a seat. [1-52-3]

verses.sanskrit: प्रतिगृह्य तु ताम् पूजाम् वसिष्ठात् राज सत्तमः ।तपो अग्नि होत्र शिष्येषु कुशलम् पर्यपृच्छत ॥१-५२-४॥
verses.text:
On receiving that deference from Vashishta, then that unsurpassed king Vishvamitra asked after the well-being 
of the ascesis, Fire-rituals and disciples and their progress in that hermitage, and that great-resplendent 
Vishvamitra has also asked after the well being of flora and fauna of the hermitage, and Vashishta reported 
to the matchless king about the all round well-being of every activity. [1-52-4, 5]

verses.sanskrit: विश्वामित्रो महातेजा वनस्पति गणे तथा ।सर्वत्र कुशलम् च आह वसिष्ठो राज सत्तमम् ॥१-५२-५॥
सुख उपविष्टम् राजानम् विश्वामित्रम् महातपाः ।पप्रच्छ जपताम् श्रेष्ठो वसिष्ठो ब्रह्मणः सुतः ॥१-५२-६॥
verses.text:
Vashishta the supreme one among meditators, a great-ascetic, and the brainchild of Brahma has then asked 
Vishvamitra who is sitting at ease. [1-52-6]

verses.sanskrit: कच्चित् ते कुशलम् राजन् कच्चित् धर्मेण रंजयन् ।प्रजाः पालयसे राजन् राज वृत्तेन धार्मिक ॥१-५२-७॥
verses.text:
I hope that you are fine! And oh, righteous king, hope that you are ruling people righteously with 
kingly righteousness to their contentment. [1-52-7]

verses.sanskrit: कच्चित् ते सुभृता भृत्याः कच्चित् तिष्ठन्ति शासने ।कच्चित् ते विजिताः सर्वे रिपवो रिपु सूदन ॥१-५२-८॥
verses.text:
I hope your servants are looked after well, hope all of them are abiding by the rulership, oh, vanquisher 
of adversaries, I hope that you have surely vanquished all of your adversaries. [1-52-8]

verses.sanskrit: कच्चित् बले च कोशे च मित्रेषु च परंतप ।कुशलम् ते नर व्याघ्र पुत्र पौत्रे तथा अनघ ॥१-५२-९॥
verses.text:
"For you are an impeccable one and an emery-inflamer, oh, tigerly-man, I hope that your forces, exchequer 
and confederates are fine, and all is well with your sons and grandsons." Thus Vashishta asked the well-being 
of Vishvamitra. [1-52-9]

verses.sanskrit: सर्वत्र कुशलम् राजा वसिष्ठम् प्रत्युदाहरत् ।विश्वामित्रो महातेजा वसिष्ठम् विनय अन्वितम् ॥१-५२-१०॥
verses.text:
The great-resplendent king Vishvamitra respectfully replied Sage Vashishta saying, 'everything 
is fine.' [1-52-10]

verses.sanskrit: कृत्वा तौ सुचिरम् कालम् धर्मिष्ठौ ताः कथाः तदा ।मुदा परमया युक्तौ प्रीयेताम् तौ परस्परम् ॥१-५२-११॥
verses.text:
Both of those celebrities of conscientious then exchanged pleasantries for a long time with their rejoice 
heightening and gladdening one another. [1-52-11]

verses.sanskrit: ततो वसिष्ठो भगवान् कथा अन्ते रघुनंदन ।विश्वामित्रम् इदम् वाक्यम् उवाच प्रहसन् इव ॥१-५२-१२॥
verses.text:
Then at the end of discussions, oh, Rama the legatee of Raghu, that godly Sage Vashishta smilingly 
spoke this sentence to Vishvamitra. [1-52-12]

verses.sanskrit: आतिथ्यम् कर्तुम् इच्छामि बलस्य अस्य महाबल ।तव च एव अप्रमेयस्य यथा अर्हम् संप्रतीच्छ मे ॥१-५२-१३॥
verses.text:
I wish to offer guestship befitting to your status, oh, great-forceful king Vishvamitra, to a matchless 
one like you and to this military force of yours, kindly accept it from me. [1-52-13]

verses.sanskrit: सत्क्रियाम् तु भवान् एताम् प्रतीच्छतु मया कृताम् ।राजन् त्वम् अतिथि श्रेष्ठः पूजनीयः प्रयत्नतः ॥१-५२-१४॥
verses.text:
'You may kindly accept all these hospitalities offered by me, oh king, for you are an important guest 
you ought to be reverenced effortfully.' So said Vashishta to Vishvamitra. [1-52-14]

verses.sanskrit: एवम् उक्तो वसिष्ठेन विश्वामित्रो महामतिः ।कृतम् इति अब्रवीत् राजा पूजा वाक्येन मे त्वया ॥१-५२-१५॥
verses.text:
When sage Vashishta said in this way, that highly-intellectual king Vishvamitra said to him, 'hospitality 
is deemed to have been offered to me with your reverential words, it is enough and nothing more is necessary.' 
[1-52-15]

verses.sanskrit: फल मूलेन भगवन् विद्यते यत् तव आश्रमे ।पाद्येन आचमनीयेन भगवद् दर्शनेन च ॥१-५२-१६॥
verses.text:
I am entertained with whatever item that obtains in your hermitage, say fruits, tubers and water 
for feet-wash and to wet mouth. Why those petty items, I regaled just by seeing you, a godly sage like. 
Oh, highly noetic sage, by yourself you are a sage worthy to be reverenced by one and all, such as you 
are you alone have reverenced me. Now I wish to take leave, please accept my obeisance to you, and please 
look upon us with a friendlily regard. [1-52-16, 17]

verses.sanskrit: सर्वथा च महाप्राज्ञ पूजा अर्हेण सुपूजितः ।नमस्ते अस्तु गमिष्यामि मैत्रेण ईक्षस्व चक्षुषा ॥१-५२-१७॥
एवम् ब्रुवन्तम् राजानम् वसिष्ठः पुनः एव हि ।न्यमंत्रयत धर्मात्मा पुनः पुनः उदार धीः ॥१-५२-१८॥
verses.text:
Even if the king was speaking in this way obliquely to him, that virtue-souled and magnanimous sage 
Vashishta entreated the king repeatedly. [1-52-18]

verses.sanskrit: बाढम् इति एव गाधेयो वसिष्ठम् प्रत्युवाच ह ।यथा प्रियम् भगवतः तथा अस्तु मुनि सत्तम ॥१-५२-१९॥
verses.text:
Gaadhi's son Vishvamitra then replied the eminent saint Vashishta, "Agreed! As it pleases the godly 
sage. So be it!" [1-52-19]

verses.sanskrit: एवम् उक्तः तथा तेन वसिष्ठो जपताम् वरः ।आजुहाव ततः प्रीतः कल्माषीम् धूत कल्मषः ॥१-५२-२०॥
verses.text:
When Vishvamitra said that way then Vashishta, the best one among meditators, and one whose blemishes 
are laved is gladdened, and started calling his speckled cow, namely Shabala, yclept Kaamadhenu. [1-52-20]

verses.sanskrit: एहि एहि शबले क्षिप्रम् शृणु च अपि वचो मम ।सबलस्य अस्य राजर्षेः कर्तुम् व्यवसितो अस्मि अहम् ।भोजनेन महा अर्हेण सत्कारम् संविधत्स्व मे ॥१-५२-२१॥
verses.text:
Come on, oh, Shabala! Come quickly and listen to my words. I prepared to accord hospitality with 
a highly delectable royal banquet to this kingly sage together with all his forces. You arrange it for 
me. [1-52-21]

verses.sanskrit: यस्य यस्य यथा कामम् षड् रसेषु अभिपूजितम् ।तत् सर्वम् काम धुक् दिव्ये अभिवर्ष कृते मम ॥१-५२-२२॥
verses.text:
Oh, Kaamadhenu, the Divine Cow, the Milker of All-Desires, for my sake you shower whatsoever foodstuff 
is savoured by whosoever according to one's own piquancy from each of the six savour, that too plentifully. 
[1-52-22]

verses.sanskrit: रसेन अन्नेन पानेन लेह्य चोष्येण संयुतम् ।अन्नानाम् निचयम् सर्वम् सृजस्व शबले त्वर ॥१-५२-२३॥
verses.text:
" 'Oh, Shabala, you create heaps of savourily foodstuffs comprised of masticable items like crunchy 
foods, drinkable items like soft-drinks, tasteable items like honey-like viscous dishes, squeezable 
items like pulpy fruits, and be quick.' " So said Sage Vashishta to that Sacred Cow, Kaamadhenu, and 
Vishvamitra continued his narration. [1-52-23]
